evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: reliable; data quality and security; pipeline reliability and monitoring
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour dominates: emphasis on reliability, quality, security, and monitoring with no velocity signals.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Procurement operations and AI—moderately risky but not explicitly financial/compliance/regulatory.
evidence.data_team_maturity: scalable ETL pipelines and data products; Databricks; established governance
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Mid: named tools (dbt, Databricks, Power BI) and established platform requirements.
evidence.jd_authorship: develop, operate, and maintain automated, scalable ETL pipelines; translating business requirements into data models; ensuring data quality and security
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Mixed: specific technical work (dbt, Databricks, DevOps) alongside generic supporting language.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: internal data products; business requirements; analytics requests
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Internal data infrastructure supporting business and analytics teams.
evidence.autonomy_level: develop, operate, and maintain; supporting analytics requests; collaborating
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Execution: support-oriented language without strategic direction verbs.

evidence.testing_framing: ensuring data quality; pipeline reliability and monitoring
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality is a named responsibility but without explicit testing framework or ownership verb.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: data quality and security; pipeline reliability; monitoring
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: operational quality and reliability are concerns but not compliance/risk-driven.
